channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: With 2 women running, the New Hampshire governor's race is both close and personal
item.description: None of the nation’s 12 female governors are up for reelection, but five women are competing as major party gubernatorial nominees in four states.
item.dc:creator: Holly Ramer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Abortion bans are top of mind for young women in North Carolina as they consider Harris or Trump
item.description: TikTok videos of women turned away from emergency rooms because of state abortion bans haunt one young female voter in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz And Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: O'Reilly: Harris wrong about Trump's Hitler comments made to Kelly
item.description: "They (Trump's comments) don't mean anything, and he doesn't even remember them 30 seconds later, O'Reilly said on "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Harris to give her campaign's closing argument at the Ellipse, where Trump helped spark Capitol riot
item.description: ASTON, Pa. (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris plans to lay out her campaign's closing argument by returning to the site near the White House where Donald Trump helped incite a mob that attacked the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris says Trump 'is a fascist' after John Kelly says he wanted generals like Hitler's
item.description: ASTON, Pa. (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris said Wednesday that she believes that Donald Trump "is a fascist” after his longest-serving chief of staff said the former president praised Adolf Hitler while in office and put personal loyalty above the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: DAN MERICA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PolitiFact weighs in on false claims about Walz and how to spot disinformation
item.description: A senior U.S. intelligence official has found that Russia is behind viral disinformation about Gov. Tim Walz. An account on X spread false claims against the Democratic vice presidential nominee but a closer look at the posts reveals inconsistencies and inaccuracies.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Ngoc Bui, MPR News

item.title: Georgia voter role audit finds 20 non-citizens registered
item.description: None has cast a ballot in November’s general election, but nine of the 20 had voted in previous elections and the other 11 had no record of voting.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics: Harris surrogates bedeviled by gaffes
item.description: Get the latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Axelrod: Harris in a 'terrible place' on Middle East conflict
item.description: Veteran Democratic strategist David Axelrod says Vice President Harris is caught in a “terrible place” on the growing conflict in the Middle East, which threatens to turn off Lebanese and Palestinian American voters in Michigan, a crucial battleground state.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin sees high turnout on the first day of in-person absentee voting
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — More than 97,000 people in Wisconsin cast absentee ballots in person on the first day they could, leading to long waits at some polling sites that were made worse by an overwhelmed computer system that clerks use to process ballots.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tim Walz, wife and son vote early in Minnesota
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — The Democratic vice presidential candidate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz, his wife, Gwen, and their son, Gus, went to a polling place in St. Paul on a crisp fall Wednesday morning to cast their ballots early. It was the first time voting for Gus, who just turned 18.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS identifies hundreds of migrants with possible ties to bloodthirsty Venezuelan gang
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security has found 600 migrants with possible ties to Tren de Aragua, and has recommended that over 100 be put on an FBI watchlist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Conservative beer company expands product line with 'perfect drink' in border-themed parody video
item.description: Ultra Right beer company announced this week that it is unveiling a new tequila product that will contribute proceeds to fight for conservative causes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: G7 allies are moving ahead with a $50 billion loan for Ukraine backed by frozen Russian funds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ukraine will receive $50 billion in loans, backed by frozen Russian assets, from Group of Seven allies, the White House said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris likens Trump to Hitler in scathing remarks, says he wants same military loyalty as German dictator
item.description: Vice President Harris, homing on a recent John Kelly interview, claimed President Trump wants "unchecked power" and a military "loyal to him personally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Renew it or lose it; lawmaker asks voters not to skip ballot question on lottery amendment
item.description: This fall, Minnesotans will vote on whether to renew a proposal earmarking some money from the state lottery to fund environmental programs, including parks projects, conservation and clean water efforts.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Democrats warn of threat to democracy in closing pitch to voters
item.description: Democrats are zeroing in on the future of democracy and former President Trump’s response to the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol as part of their closing pitch to voters two weeks out from Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans brace for leadership scramble
item.description: House Republicans are bracing for potential leadership shake-ups in the coming weeks, a post-election scramble that will heavily depend on who wins control of the lower chamber and White House in November. The biggest wildcard is what Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will do.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Arvin council seat to remain vacant for two years
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- The shakeup might be over for Arvin City Council after council members finally made a decision on the vacant seat.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Treviño, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump hurls a string of insults at Harris including 'lazy,' a racist trope against Black people
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (AP) — Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump unleashed a series of personal attacks at Vice President Kamala Harris, calling her “lazy” — a word long used to demean Black people in racist terms — and repeatedly questioning her intelligence and stamina.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump tells supporters to 'just vote' at lively Georgia rally organized by Charlie Kirk
item.description: Republican Donald Trump is pushing supporters in Georgia to get out and vote for him in a state that could be crucial in the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: American Airlines fined $50 million for its treatment of passengers with disabilities
item.description: The U.S. Department of Transportation penalty against American Airlines comes after "numerous serious violations of the laws protecting airline passengers with disabilities" over a four-year period.
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis, MPR News

item.title: Judge orders Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani must turn over much of his property — his apartment in New York, the city that made him mayor, and other prized possessions — to two Georgia election workers who have sought for months to collect their massive defamation judgment, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, PIX 11

item.title: Jailed reporters, silenced networks: What Trump says he'd do to the media if elected
item.description: On the campaign trail, former President Donald Trump has threatened to jail reporters who won't give up the sources of leaks and to strip networks of their broadcast licenses for fact-checking him.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: These are the 2 paths either Trump or Harris has to follow to become president
item.description: With seven states up for grabs, here are the likely scenarios that would put either candidate in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Harris will campaign in Texas to highlight state's abortion ban in a pitch to battleground voters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will head to reliably Republican Texas just 10 days before Election Day in an effort to refocus her campaign against former President Donald Trump on reproductive care, which Democrats see as a make-or-break issue this year.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rudy Giuliani ordered to turn over NYC apartment, 26 watches to Georgia election workers
item.description: A federal judge has ordered Rudy Giuliani to turn over his New York City apartment and various other assets to two Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Missouri woman finds trunk full of stolen campaign signs with help of Apple AirTag
item.description: "I expected to find the AirTag, but not 59 signs. It was kind of like finding a dead body," the Missouri woman said.
item.dc:creator: Parker Padgett, NewsNation

item.title: Jill Douglas cites education and crime as key issues in Nevada State Senate race
item.description: Jill Douglas, a Republican running for State Senate District 6, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform as she challenges for the seat.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rejects Mark Meadows attempt to get White House records for Georgia defense
item.description: A federal judge cast aside former White House chief of staff Mark Meadows's bid for a cache of records from the National Archives meant to aid in his defense against criminal charges in the Georgia election case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation
